note: INVALID TEST: Represents a damaged/disposed card with an unreadable serial — matcher should reject, not guess.
ISSUED TEST: Second issued-status card to confirm SR escalation path, not a top-up.
FRAUD TEST: Already used voucher to test rejection.
LOCKED TEST: Reported stolen/lost — customer directed to point of purchase, not topped up.
DECOY: Used to prove the Matcher resolves Fuzzy Serial Collisions using the PIN.
INVALID TEST: Represents a badly printed/washed voucher — should route to reprint, not recovery.
LOCKED TEST: Locked after over-scratched top-up procedure; return to point of purchase.
FRAUD TEST: Same batch as another used voucher, checks batch-level abuse detection.
EXPIRY TEST: Card is physically expired, beyond the 6-month replacement window.
RANGE TEST: Companion to the above — adjacent serial should NOT be auto-matched to the wrong PIN.
EXPIRY TEST: Expired more than 6 months ago — NOT eligible for replacement.
EXPIRY TEST: Expired within the last 6 months — eligible for retail replacement.
RANGE TEST: Serial deliberately close (+/-15) to another active serial, to test range-collision handling.
ISSUED TEST: Card printed/allocated but not yet active — CEE must raise an SR, not top up.
